key_points: The East India Company came to India mainly for trade in spices, cotton, silk and other goods
Gradually the Company used military power and political influence to gain control over Indian territories
The Battle of Plassey in 1757 marked the beginning of British political power in India
After the Battle of Buxar in 1764, the Company received the Diwani rights to collect revenue from Bengal
The British expanded their rule using policies such as Subsidiary Alliance and the Doctrine of Lapse
examples: Robert Clive led the British in the Battle of Plassey
Siraj-ud-Daulah was the Nawab of Bengal defeated by the British
Tipu Sultan of Mysore fought against British expansion
Several Indian states were annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse
formulas: British expansion process → Trade → Military victories → Political control → Territorial rule
Battle sequence → Plassey (1757) → Buxar (1764) → Diwani rights (1765)
Expansion methods → Wars + Alliances + Annexation policies
exam_tips: Remember the importance of the Battles of Plassey and Buxar
Learn the meaning of Diwani, Subsidiary Alliance and Doctrine of Lapse
Understand how the East India Company changed from traders to rulers
Use examples of important rulers such as Tipu Sultan and the Marathas in answers
